report_name: European Ransomware Incidents Surge by 55% in Early 2026, Black Kite Report Finds

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: Identified as the most prolific ransomware group in Europe during early 2026, with 372 incidents across 26 countries. The group operates a Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) model and employs double extortion tactics.

threat_actors.name: Akira
threat_actors.description: The second most active ransomware group in Europe during early 2026, with 159 incidents. The group employs double extortion tactics.

threat_actors.name: SafePay
threat_actors.description: A notable ransomware group demonstrating a highly targeted campaign with a strong focus on German organizations.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: Used to compromise smaller, less secure suppliers to gain access to larger, primary targets as part of supply chain attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Core technique used by ransomware groups like Qilin and Akira to encrypt victim systems, halting operations and demanding a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Used as part of a double extortion strategy to exfiltrate victim data to cloud storage before encryption, adding pressure to pay the ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware operators use tools like vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, preventing system recovery from local backups. The command `vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet` was noted as a key indicator.

attack_patterns.name: Brute Force
attack_patterns.description: Indicated as a common initial access vector, detectable by a high volume of failed login attempts (Windows Security Log Event ID 4625).

malware.name: Qilin Ransomware
malware.description: Ransomware payload operated by the Qilin group, likely as part of a Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) model. It is used to encrypt victim data and exfiltrate it for double extortion.

malware.name: Akira Ransomware
malware.description: Ransomware payload operated by the Akira group. It is used to encrypt victim data and exfiltrate it for double extortion.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Abused by ransomware operators to delete volume shadow copies using the command `vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet` to inhibit system recovery.

tools.name: WMIC
tools.description: Legitimate Windows tool often abused by ransomware for various malicious activities, such as disabling security software or lateral movement.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Legitimate Windows tool often abused by ransomware for various malicious activities, such as disabling security software or lateral movement.

identities.name: Manufacturing Sector
identities.description: The most heavily targeted industry in Europe during early 2026, accounting for 28% of all ransomware incidents.

infrastructure.name: Ransomware Leak Site
infrastructure.description: Used by double extortion ransomware groups like Qilin and Akira to publish stolen victim data, increasing pressure to pay the ransom.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8a1b9c-3d4e-4f5a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/increase-ransomware-europe/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
      - '/all'
      - '/quiet'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the command `vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet`, which is a classic ransomware technique to prevent system recovery.

indicators.name: Suspicious LOLBin Execution for Ransomware Activity
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Suspicious LOLBin Execution for Ransomware Activity
id: 9c8d7e6f-5a4b-4c3d-2b1a-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate Windows tools (LOLBins) that are commonly abused by ransomware for defense evasion, lateral movement, and inhibiting system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/increase-ransomware-europe/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1047
  - attack.t1490
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\vssadmin.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PowerShell, WMIC, or VSSAdmin, which are legitimate Windows tools often abused by ransomware operators.

indicators.name: High Volume of Failed Logon Attempts
indicators.pattern:
title: High Volume of Failed Logon Attempts
id: 5a4b3c2d-1e0f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4d3c2b1a
status: experimental
description: Detects a high volume of failed logon attempts (Event ID 4625), which can indicate a brute-force or password spraying attack.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/increase-ransomware-europe/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1110
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4625
  condition: selection | count() > 20 by SourceNetworkAddress within 5m
indicators.description: Detects a high number of failed logon attempts from a single source, potentially indicating a brute-force or password spraying attack, as mentioned as a common initial access vector.

relationships.source_name: High Volume of Failed Logon Attempts
